report_name: CISA Warns of 'ShadowProxy' Phishing-as-a-Service that Bypasses MFA

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: The ShadowProxy platform enables attackers to send phishing emails containing a link that directs victims to the ShadowProxy reverse proxy server instead of the legitimate service.

attack_patterns.name: Adversary-in-the-Middle
attack_patterns.description: ShadowProxy operates as an adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) reverse proxy, intercepting traffic between the victim and the legitimate cloud service to steal credentials and session cookies.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Interception
attack_patterns.description: The ShadowProxy platform intercepts the MFA challenge sent to the user and the user's response, passing it to the legitimate service to complete the authentication process, which then generates the session cookie for theft.

attack_patterns.name: Steal Web Session Cookie
attack_patterns.description: The ShadowProxy reverse proxy server intercepts the session cookie generated by a legitimate service after a successful MFA login, stealing it before it reaches the victim's browser.

attack_patterns.name: Use Alternate Authentication Material
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use the stolen session cookie by injecting it into their own browser to hijack the victim's authenticated session and gain access to their account without needing to re-authenticate.

malware.name: ShadowProxy
malware.description: A Phishing-as-a-Service (PhaaS) platform sold on dark web forums that uses an adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) reverse proxy to steal credentials and session cookies, enabling attackers to bypass multi-factor authentication.

identities.name: Microsoft 365 Users
identities.description: Users of Microsoft 365 cloud services targeted by the ShadowProxy PhaaS platform for credential and session hijacking.

identities.name: Google Workspace Users
identities.description: Users of Google Workspace cloud services targeted by the ShadowProxy PhaaS platform for credential and session hijacking.

infrastructure.name: ShadowProxy Reverse Proxy Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: The reverse proxy server infrastructure used by the ShadowProxy platform to perform adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) attacks, presenting a fake login page to victims and intercepting credentials and session cookies.

indicators.name: Azure AD Sign-in from Anonymous Proxy
indicators.pattern:
title: Azure AD Sign-in from Anonymous Proxy
id: 2b8c1f9a-5d7e-4b0f-8c6a-9e3d1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects successful Azure AD sign-ins originating from an IP address associated with an anonymous proxy service. This can be an indicator of an attacker using a stolen session cookie from an AiTM phishing attack like ShadowProxy.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-26
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/26/joint-advisory-on-shadowproxy-phaas
tags:
  - attack.t1090.002
  - attack.t1539
  - attack.defense_evasion
  - attack.credential_access
level: high
logsource:
  product: azure
  service: signinlogs
detection:
  selection:
    ResultType: 0
    RiskDetail: 'anonymizedIpAddress'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects successful Azure AD sign-ins originating from an IP address associated with an anonymous proxy service, which can indicate the use of a stolen session cookie from an AiTM phishing attack.

indicators.name: Potential AiTM Phishing Site Access
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential AiTM Phishing Site Access
id: 8f9e0d1c-3b2a-4f5e-8a9b-0c1d2e3f4a5b
status: experimental
description: Detects web traffic to typosquatted or lookalike domains mimicking major cloud service login pages, a key component of Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing attacks used by platforms like ShadowProxy.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-26
references:
  - https://www.cisa.gov/news-events/alerts/2026/04/26/joint-advisory-on-shadowproxy-phaas
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
level: high
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|contains:
      - 'login-microsft'
      - 'microsftonline'
      - 'office36S'
      - 'gogle-accounts'
  filter:
    cs-host|endswith:
      - '.microsoft.com'
      - '.google.com'
      - '.live.com'
  condition: selection and not filter
indicators.description: Detects web traffic to typosquatted or lookalike domains mimicking major cloud service login pages, a key component of Adversary-in-the-Middle (AiTM) phishing attacks.

relationships.source_name: Azure AD Sign-in from Anonymous Proxy
